title: phyllanthus simplex var. minutiflorus
intro: Phyllanthus simplex var. minutiflorus, also known as the minute-flowered phyllanthus, is a plant species in the family Phyllanthaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and greenish-yellow foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Phyllanthus simplex var. minutiflorus are said to include the reduction of fever, relief from respiratory issues, and the promotion of digestive health. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by making a tea from its leaves or using it in combination with other herbs to create a tonic or infusion.
